title: Chamaeiris monnieri
intro:
I couldn't find any information on Chamaeiris monnieri. However, based on the name, it seems to be a plant that could be related to the Indian snakeroot (Ageratina alnifolia) or the genus Chamaeiris which seems to be a synonym for the genus Chamaemelum. 

If Chamaeiris is a synonym for the genus Chamaemelum, then the following information might be relevant.

Chamaemelum, commonly known as Chamaeiris, is a genus of flowering plants in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The health benefits of Chamaemelum include its use as a calming agent and its potential to reduce anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Chamaemelum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Chamaemelum include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
